title: Blink and You Missed It
subtitle: How long people actually look at your profile
context: The average user spends just 3.2 seconds deciding whether to swipe left or right on a profile. That is less time than it takes to read this sentence. Your first photo is not just important; it is everything.
comparison: By comparison, the average person spends 7 seconds deciding what to order at a coffee shop.

title: Tuesday Night Fever
subtitle: When the swiping really heats up
context: Forget Friday nights. The biggest spike in dating-app activity happens on Tuesdays at 8 PM, right in that sweet spot between dinner and doom-scrolling. Across the Nordics, Tuesday consistently beats every other day for new matches, first messages, and profile updates.
comparison: Sunday at 10 PM is the second-most active time, fueled by the collective dread of the work week ahead.

title: Paws and Effect
subtitle: The secret weapon hiding in your photo gallery
statLabel: of first messages mention a pet
context: If your profile includes a pet photo, nearly three-quarters of your incoming first messages will reference the animal. Dogs are the ultimate icebreaker. People will ask about your golden retriever before they ask about you. Accept it.
comparison: Only 12% of first messages reference a person's job, and 8% mention a travel photo.
